title: Capturing Structural Locality in Non-parametric Language Models

structured.summary: This paper proposes incorporating manually defined structural locality features into the retrieval process of non-parametric kNN language models to improve neighbor relevance and language modeling performance. By learning simple linear adjustments to distances based on hierarchical locality categories, the method achieves modest perplexity and accuracy improvements on Wikipedia text and Java source code datasets without retraining the base model.

strengths.point: Addresses an important limitation of current non-parametric LMs by explicitly modeling structural locality
strengths.evidence: The paper identifies that existing distance metrics insufficiently capture hierarchical or topical relationships inherent in datasets like Wikipedia and source code.

strengths.point: Simple and parameter-efficient approach to integrate locality with minimal overhead
strengths.evidence: Only two parameters per locality level are learned via validation set optimization, requiring no retraining of the underlying neural LM.

strengths.point: Demonstrates consistent, though modest, improvements across two diverse, real-world domains
strengths.evidence: Experiments show relative perplexity reductions of 2-4% and top-k accuracy gains on WIKITEXT-103 and a large Java GitHub corpus.

strengths.point: Provides insightful analysis on the interaction between neural context distance and structural locality
strengths.evidence: The paper empirically shows that locality signals are complementary to learned embeddings and improve neighbor ranking.

weaknesses.point: Lacks comparison to recent neural retrieval augmentation frameworks that learn locality or memory structures end-to-end
weaknesses.evidence: No discussion or experiments comparing to methods like REALM or RAG that incorporate structural metadata in retrieval.

weaknesses.point: Manual and heuristic design of locality categories without ablation or sensitivity analysis
weaknesses.evidence: The paper does not investigate how different granularities or automated locality feature discovery would affect results.

weaknesses.point: Simplistic linear parameterization of distance adjustment lacks justification or exploration of alternatives
weaknesses.evidence: No experiments test nonlinear functions or more expressive models to capture complex locality-distance interactions.

weaknesses.point: Evaluation limited to perplexity and token-level accuracy without downstream task or human evaluation
weaknesses.evidence: No assessments on practical applications such as code completion benchmarks or text generation quality.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient details and analysis on reproducibility and computational overhead
weaknesses.evidence: The paper omits datastore construction specifics, retrieval efficiency metrics, and training hyperparameters for learning locality parameters.

weaknesses.point: No exploration of scenarios lacking clear structural metadata or cases where locality incorporation fails
weaknesses.evidence: The method’s applicability beyond well-structured domains like Wikipedia and source code remains untested.

weaknesses.point: No statistical significance testing or error bars reported for observed improvements
weaknesses.evidence: Tables report absolute metrics without confidence intervals or variance estimates.

questions.question: How does the proposed locality weighting compare empirically to learned neural retrieval augmentations that model structural or hierarchical metadata?
questions.motivation: Understanding comparative benefits is essential to position this method within the broader landscape of retrieval-augmented language modeling.

questions.question: Can the manual locality categories be replaced or supplemented by automated methods to discover meaningful locality features?
questions.motivation: Reducing reliance on domain expertise would enhance generalizability and ease adoption in diverse or less structured datasets.

questions.question: How does the locality-based distance adjustment affect retrieval latency and scalability in very large datastores?
questions.motivation: Practical deployment requires knowledge of computational trade-offs introduced by the method.

questions.question: Are there contexts or datasets where incorporating structural locality might degrade performance, and how robust is the method to noisy or ambiguous locality signals?
questions.motivation: Understanding failure modes informs safe application and further improvements.

critique_points.point_001: Lacks comparison to recent neural retrieval augmentation frameworks that learn locality or memory structures end-to-end. No discussion or experiments comparing to methods like REALM or RAG that incorporate structural metadata in retrieval
critique_points.point_002: Manual and heuristic design of locality categories without ablation or sensitivity analysis. The paper does not investigate how different granularities or automated locality feature discovery would affect results
critique_points.point_003: Simplistic linear parameterization of distance adjustment lacks justification or exploration of alternatives. No experiments test nonlinear functions or more expressive models to capture complex locality-distance interactions
critique_points.point_004: Evaluation limited to perplexity and token-level accuracy without downstream task or human evaluation. No assessments on practical applications such as code completion benchmarks or text generation quality
critique_points.point_005: Insufficient details and analysis on reproducibility and computational overhead. The paper omits datastore construction specifics, retrieval efficiency metrics, and training hyperparameters for learning locality parameters
critique_points.point_006: No exploration of scenarios lacking clear structural metadata or cases where locality incorporation fails. The method’s applicability beyond well-structured domains like Wikipedia and source code remains untested
critique_points.point_007: No statistical significance testing or error bars reported for observed improvements. Tables report absolute metrics without confidence intervals or variance estimates
